globalDisclaimer: This is not a medical diagnosis. It is a rough read of your answers today. Real life is more complex than five scores.
confidenceNote: If every score looks very high or very low, try again when you can answer honestly, not when you are tired or trying to look a certain way.

low.summary: You like what works. New ideas can feel distracting until they prove useful.
strengths: You focus on what is practical
You help teams avoid chasing every new idea
You spot when a plan will not work in real life
blindSpots: You may say no to a good idea because it feels unfamiliar
Others may think you are negative when you are being careful
low.contextCaveat: Under stress, you may stick to what you know. That helps until the situation really needs change.

typical.summary: You can be curious without needing constant change.
strengths: You balance new ideas with follow-through
You try new things when they have a clear point
You stay engaged without needing excitement every day
blindSpots: Some weeks you lean more practical, some weeks more creative
People may read you differently depending on the day
typical.contextCaveat: This trait shifts with boredom, stress, and who you are around.

high.summary: Ideas interest you. You notice details and possibilities others skip.
strengths: You connect ideas across topics
You enjoy learning for its own sake
Unclear situations do not scare you as much
blindSpots: You may start new projects before finishing old ones
You may like change more than upkeep
high.contextCaveat: Works well in creative or research roles. Harder in jobs that rarely allow change.

low.summary: You prefer flexibility over strict plans. Too much structure can feel limiting.
strengths: You adapt when plans fall apart
You move on when perfect is not needed
You bring energy when things are unclear
blindSpots: Deadlines can slip when no one is tracking them
Small tasks can pile up quietly
low.contextCaveat: You often do better when you care about the task. Boring tasks are harder to finish.

typical.summary: You can plan when you need to, but you do not want your whole life on a checklist.
strengths: You finish important work without over-organizing everything
People can usually count on you in normal weeks
You know when to tighten up and when to relax
blindSpots: Heavy stress can break your routines fast
You may blame yourself for laziness when you are actually overloaded
typical.contextCaveat: Stress is the real test, not your best day.

high.summary: You like tasks done, clear, and in order. Loose ends bother you.
strengths: You finish long projects
People trust you with details
You reduce chaos for yourself and others
blindSpots: You may spend too long making things perfect
Late changes are harder for you than for others
high.contextCaveat: High standards help until they never let you rest.

low.summary: People can drain your energy even when you like them. Alone time helps you recharge.
strengths: You think before you speak
You listen well
You focus deeply when working alone
blindSpots: Good ideas may stay quiet in loud groups
Small talk can feel tiring
low.contextCaveat: You can be social when you need to. It just may not refill your energy.

typical.summary: You are not always quiet and not always outgoing. It depends on the situation.
strengths: You can join a group without needing attention
You recover if you get quiet time after social time
You choose depth over small talk when it matters
blindSpots: People may label you differently in different settings
You may overbook social plans, then feel worn out
typical.contextCaveat: Energy here depends on sleep, stress, and whether you want to be there.

high.summary: Talking and people often give you energy. Long silence can feel draining.
strengths: You warm up a room
You start conversations others avoid
You build momentum in groups
blindSpots: You may talk over quieter people
You may feel bored when alone too long
high.contextCaveat: People-facing roles fit well. Deep solo work still needs breaks.

low.summary: You will say the hard thing when needed. Being liked is not always your top goal.
strengths: You push back on bad group decisions
You negotiate without giving in too fast
You protect your boundaries
blindSpots: You may sound harsher than you mean
Warmth may not show on your face even when you care
low.contextCaveat: Helpful in tough decisions. Can create friction in sensitive relationships.

typical.summary: You can work with others and still stand your ground.
strengths: You look for fair compromises
You care how people feel, within reason
You can be kind or firm depending on the situation
blindSpots: Under pressure you may give in too much or push too hard
You may stay quiet to keep peace, then feel resentful
typical.contextCaveat: Notice what you gave up to avoid a fight.

high.summary: You care about other people's feelings. You prefer peace over winning an argument.
strengths: You calm tense situations
People trust you quickly
You remember the human side of decisions
blindSpots: You may delay hard conversations
You may carry problems that are not yours
high.contextCaveat: Kindness helps until people ignore your needs.

low.summary: Stress affects you, but it usually does not take over your day.
strengths: You recover from setbacks faster
You do not assume the worst from every problem
You can handle uncertainty without spiraling
blindSpots: You may not see how stressed someone else is
You may call their worry an overreaction
low.contextCaveat: Calm does not mean you should ignore real problems.

typical.summary: Some weeks feel steady. Some weeks feel harder. That is normal.
strengths: You notice problems early enough to fix them
You do not pretend life is always easy
You can calm down when you have time and support
blindSpots: Sleep, caffeine, and overload change this fast
You may judge yourself for reacting when you are just stressed
typical.contextCaveat: The same person can score differently in a different month.

high.summary: Worry and mood shifts show up more strongly for you. Stress can feel loud and long-lasting.
strengths: You catch mistakes before they grow
You notice emotional tension in yourself and others
You do not need fake positivity
blindSpots: Small problems can replay in your head
Criticism can hurt longer than you want
high.contextCaveat: This is a tendency, not a life sentence. Skills and support can change how it feels.
